query: As of July 2026, financial and bankruptcy status of these companies: Chegg Inc, America's Car-Mart Inc, JetBlue Airways, Peloton Inc, Faraday Future Intelligent Electric, Petco Health and Wellness. Which are at highest risk of bankruptcy filing in mid-2026? Any going concern warnings, debt maturities, or restructuring news?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the findings:

## Bankruptcy Risk Ranking (July 2026)

**Highest risk — explicit going-concern warnings:**

1. **America's Car-Mart (CRMT)** — Most acute. Under a **forbearance agreement** with Silver Point Finance, $892M debt, nine-month FY2026 net loss of $104.9M, Houlihan Lokey engaged for a ≥$500M capital raise, closing 42 stores (~one-third of footprint). Filings explicitly warn of possible bankruptcy and total loss for shareholders. Stock down ~95% from its 52-week high.

2. **Faraday Future (FFAI)** — Substantial going-concern doubt with only **~$12M cash**, near-zero revenue ($512K in Q1), Nasdaq delisting process (deadline Sept 16, 2026), and management explicitly citing financing as needed to avoid bankruptcy. Stock at 52-week low (~$0.19).

**Moderate risk:**

3. **Chegg (CHGG)** — Revenue collapsing ~48% YoY under AI pressure; strategic review/potential sale underway. But it's been buying back its 2026 converts at a discount (only ~$34M left) and has **no going-concern warning** — bankruptcy isn't the near-term base case.

4. **JetBlue (JBLU)** — Deeply loss-making ($319M Q1 loss) with ~$755M of 2026 maturities, but ~$2.4B liquidity + $6B+ unencumbered assets provide substantial runway. Some analysts flag multi-year Chapter 11 risk, not mid-2026.

**Low risk — refinancing completed:**

5. **Petco (WOOF)** — February 2026 refinancing pushed maturities to 2028/2031; operationally profitable.

6. **Peloton (PTON)** — Completed $1.35B refinancing, cut net debt ~70%, ~$1.13B cash, expects first-ever full-year positive net income. Lowest risk.

**Bottom line:** The two companies at highest risk of a mid-2026 bankruptcy filing are **America's Car-Mart** and **Faraday Future**, with Car-Mart the most acute given its active lender forbearance and restructuring timelines. Note: AskNews was unavailable (credit limit), so qualitative news relied on web search; all figures are cited in the structured output.

sources.title: America's Car-Mart Announces Agreement with Lenders to Support Ongoing Strategic Review Process
sources.snippet: Lenders agreed to waive specified defaults and provide covenant relief through early September 2026, extendable to November 2026.

sources.title: America's Car-Mart Faces June 12 Deadline as Liquidity Pressures Mount
sources.snippet: Operating under a forbearance agreement with Silver Point Finance expiring June 12, 2026.

sources.title: America's Car-Mart Looks for Lifeline Amid Mounting Financial Struggles
sources.snippet: Houlihan Lokey marketing a potential capital raise of at least $500 million; potential need to seek bankruptcy protection.

sources.title: America's Car-Mart 10-Q Quarterly Earnings Report
sources.snippet: Nine-month net loss of $104.9M; total debt $892.2M as of Jan 31, 2026; $47.0M deferred tax valuation allowance.

sources.title: Faraday Future flags going concern risk after $42.3M Q1 loss
sources.snippet: Recurring losses, $12.2M cash, ~$76M negative working capital create substantial doubt about going concern.

sources.title: Faraday Future Announces Nasdaq Delisting Determination and Intention to Appeal
sources.snippet: Nasdaq minimum bid price deficiency; deadline Sept 16, 2026 to regain compliance.

sources.title: Chegg Repurchases $20M of 0% 2026 Convertible Notes
sources.snippet: Feb 13, 2026: repurchased $20M of 0% 2026 notes for $19.4M; ~$33.9M remained outstanding.

sources.title: Chegg to Repurchase $116.6M in Convertible Notes at Discount
sources.snippet: Repurchased ~$116.6M of 0% Convertible Senior Notes due 2026 for ~$96.2M cash.

sources.title: CHGG Q1 Earnings Call: Revenue Decline, Cost Cuts, and Strategic Alternatives
sources.snippet: Strategic alternatives process; layoffs of 248 employees (~22% of workforce); cost savings target $100-110M in 2026.

sources.title: JetBlue remains deep in the red in Q1 2026
sources.snippet: Q1 2026 net loss $319M; ~$2.4B liquidity plus revolver and $6B+ unencumbered assets; ~$755M of 2026 maturities.

sources.title: Why Chapter 11 Might Be the Best Move for JetBlue Stock in 2026
sources.snippet: Analyst discussion of elevated bankruptcy scenarios for JetBlue over multi-year horizon.

sources.title: Peloton Announces Successful Completion of $1.35 Billion Holistic Refinancing
sources.snippet: Refinancing reduced debt, extended maturities, repurchased ~$800M of 0% 2026 converts at a discount.

sources.title: Peloton posts Q3 profit with stronger cash flow (10-Q)
sources.snippet: Q3 FCF $151M; FY2026 FCF guided ~$350M; ~70% net debt reduction; ~$1.13B cash; expects first full-year positive net income.

sources.title: Petco refinances debt as Q1 sales inch higher (10-Q)
sources.snippet: $900M term loan (due Mar 2028) + $600M 8.25% notes (due Feb 2031); ~$654M total liquidity; Adjusted EBITDA $97.3M.

sources.title: Petco Announces Launch of Debt Refinancing Transaction
sources.snippet: Refinanced $1.5B term loan into $900M term loan and $600M senior secured notes.
